page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 120 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB per core, and includes 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage distributed across five drives. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and cryptographic acceleration.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores placing in the top 10% tier. Multi-core performance, measured via Geekbench and PassMark CPU tests, falls within the top 25% tier. However, multi-core stress-ng tests and memory bandwidth benchmarks, such as read-only and write operations on large blocks, remain in the average middle 50% tier. This indicates a tradeoff where single-threaded execution and specialized vector instructions outperform raw multi-threaded memory throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage directly bundled with 32 dedicated vCPUs offers high resource density for disk-I/O intensive workloads without requiring external storage attachments. The hardware profile is optimized for workloads such as database operations, text and image processing, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference, where the AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets can be fully leveraged.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 32 vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. The system includes 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across five drives, offering high local disk throughput. Its x86_64 architecture supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets, which drive top-tier single-core performance in text processing, image processing, and single-threaded compression benchmarks. While multi-core processing and memory bandwidth benchmarks perform in the average tier, the server delivers strong results in Redis operations and static web serving. This configuration provides a balanced, high-density resource profile for database operations, web serving, and localized data processing tasks without the need for external storage overhead.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is a compute-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and 1745 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd features 32 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and 1745 GB local NVMe SSD storage for compute and database workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, integrated local NVMe SSD storage, and advanced matrix execution units.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 120 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor
1745 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage across five drives
Top-tier single-core performance in compression and image processing benchmarks
Hardware-accelerated Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets
Optimized for database operations, static web serving, and text processing
